BASED ON DAMAGE PHOTOS FROM EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AND NEWS MEDIA...RADAR ANALYSIS...AND INFORMATION FROM AN EYEWITNESS...THE LOCALIZED WIND EVENT IN TEQUESTA HAS BEEN CLASSIFED AS STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE...WITH MAXIMUM WINDS NEAR 55 MPH. SEVERAL POWER-POLES WERE DAMAGED...POWER-LINES WERE DOWNED...AND A TREE WAS TOPPLED DURING A STRONG THUNDERSTORM AT 445 PM EDT.

RADAR ANALYSIS AND INFORMATION FROM EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT INDICATES STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS FROM A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED WIND DAMAGE ALONG HIGHWAY 40...JUST EAST OF HIGHWAY 19. SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWNED...SOME TOPPLED ONTO HIGHWAY 40. WINDS ESTIMATED NEAR 60 MPH. QUARTER SIZE HAIL WAS ALSO REPORTED IN THIS AREA DURING THE STORM.

F-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 1103AM JUST SOUTHEAST OF FIRE STATION 7 ON 12TH ST NEAR 82ND AVE, MOVED RAPIDLY NNW IMPACTING VERO PALM ESTATES, COUNTRYSIDE MOBILE HOME PARK AND PARADISE PARK LIFTING NEAR 86TH AVE AND 26TH ST FOR A TOTAL OF 1.6 MILES, MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 180 YARDS AND MAXIMUM WINDS OF 80 TO 85 MPH. THE TORNADO PRODUCED A DISCONTINUOUS DAMAGE PATH PRODUCING MINOR DAMAGE TO 62 STRUCTURES AND MAJOR DAMAGE TO 33 STRUCTURES. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR 2 MINUTES, LIFTING AT 1105 AM.

A CITIZEN VIDEOTAPED A WATESPOUT OVER LAKE APOPKA FOR SEVERAL MINUTES AS IT MOVED NORTHEAST FROM NEAR THE CENTER OF LAKE APOPKA TOWARD THE NORTHEAST SHORE. A SHORT TIME LATER...SEVERAL CITIZENS LOCATED A FEW MILES NORTHEAST OF THE LAKE OBSERVED THE WATERSPOUT MOVE ONSHORE AND TRACK ACROSS THE RURAL LAKE APOPKA RESTORATION AREA BEFORE DISSIPATING. THE TORNADO WAS ESTIMATED AS AN EF0 WITH A TRACK LENGTH 1.7 MILES AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 50 YARDS...OCCURRING FROM 431-434 PM.
TOP OF TREE DOWN. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
PUBLIC REPORTED TORNADO NORTH OF LAKE APOPKA AND PICTURES VIA PUBLIC AND MEDIA APPEAR TO CONFIRM WATERSPOUT OVER LAKE APOPKA.
ROOFTOPS BLOWN OFF HOMES AND POWER LINE DAMAGE. POSSIBLE TORNADO. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

THE TORNADO WHICH IMPACTED EDGEWATER FROM AT 1725 PM EST CONTINUED NORTHEAST AS A WATERSPOUT AND CROSSED THE INTRACOASTAL RIVER AND ASSOCIATED MARSH ISLANDS. A CITIZEN VIDEOTAPED THE LARGE WATESPOUT AS IT ADVANCED OVER THE RIVER...FARTHER AWAY FROM HIS VANTAGE POINT LESS THAN A MILE TO THE NORTHWEST. THE WATERSPOUT THEN MOVED ONSHORE THE BARRIER ISLAND AT BETHUNE BEACH AT 529 PM EST.

NWS DAMAGE SURVEY STILL UNDERWAY. PRELIMINARY FINDINGS INDICATE A HIGH END EF-1 TORNADO OCCURED AT APPROXIMATELY 530 PM, WITH ESTIMATED WINDS OF 95-110 MPH. PATH LENGTH APPROXIMATELY 1 MILE, WIDTH APPROXIMATELY 150 YARDS. DAMAGE FOCUSED IN TERRA MAR VILLAGE AND JONES FISH CAMP ROAD. APPROXIMATELY 40 MOBILE HOMES DAMAGED, POWER LINES DOWN AND NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. A FINAL UPDATE FROM THE SURVEY WILL BE ISSUED THIS AFTERNOON.
